Package org.areslib.command.sysid

Class SysIdRoutine

java.lang.Object
org.areslib.command.sysid.SysIdRoutine
public class SysIdRoutine extends Object
An orchestrator object for WPILib-style System Identification (SysId).

Using quasistatic (voltage ramp) and dynamic (voltage step) tests, SysId mathematically derives perfect Feedforward constants (kS, kV, kA) to give you drift-free and immediate mechanical reaction times.

    SysIdRoutine.Config

    static class 
    SysIdRoutine.Mechanism
    Binds the mechanism IO specifically for testing.

  • Constructor Details

    • SysIdRoutine

      public SysIdRoutine(SysIdRoutine.Config config, SysIdRoutine.Mechanism mechanism)
      Creates a new System Identification routine factory for a specific mechanism.
      Parameters:
      config - The routine profile configuration (voltage ramp rates, step values, timeouts).
      mechanism - The hardware IO binding for the target mechanism.

  • Method Details

    • quasistatic

      public Command quasistatic(SysIdRoutine.Direction direction)
      Creates a command to run a quasistatic (slow voltage ramp) test. Finds kS (static friction) and kV (velocity constant).
      Parameters:
      direction - The direction value.
      Returns:
      The current value.

    • dynamic

      public Command dynamic(SysIdRoutine.Direction direction)
      Creates a command to run a dynamic (instant voltage step) test. Finds kA (acceleration root constant).
      Parameters:
      direction - The direction value.
      Returns:
      The current value.
